name: Systematic Event-Driven: Earnings Surprise + Price Reaction
description: Systematically trade post-earnings announcement drift (PEAD): stocks with large positive earnings surprises continue to drift upward for 3-30 days after the announcement. The drift is stronger when: (1) the surprise is large, (2) analyst coverage is low, and (3) institutional ownership is low.
core_logic: SUE = (EPS_actual - EPS_expected) / sigma_EPS. Enter long when SUE > 1.5 AND price_gap > +3% on earnings day. Enter short when SUE < -1.5 AND price_gap < -3%. Hold 5 days for drift capture.

sharpe_ratio.note: PEAD alpha documented since 1968, persists in 2025

risk.main_risk: Earnings gaps can reverse; guidance disappointments post-initial reaction

pros: PEAD is one of the oldest documented anomalies
cons: Requires earnings calendar and EPS consensus data
Trading around earnings increases transaction costs
